articles.title: The surprising reason you’re so productive one day and not the next

articles.title: Doing this throughout life may cut Alzheimer’s risk by 38%

articles.title: Lonely people have worse memory but don’t decline faster, study finds

articles.title: Your nose could detect Alzheimer’s years before symptoms begin

articles.title: Scientists say we’ve been treating Alzheimer’s all wrong

articles.title: Scientists discover hidden gut trigger behind ALS and dementia

articles.title: Heartbeat Rhythms Unveil Hidden Brain Activity
articles.excerpt: Chaos-based heartbeat analysis can non-invasively detect mental load where conventional methods fail.

articles.title: Midlife Hobbies Outperform Genetic Alzheimer’s Risk
articles.excerpt: A 10-year longitudinal study finds that diverse lifestyle activities in midlife are more powerful than the APOE ε4 gene in shaping cognitive health.

articles.title: Redefining Neuroplasticity Through Selective Deactivation
articles.excerpt: A new study finds that the auditory cortex in deaf individuals uses selective deactivation to represent visual spatial features.

articles.title: Secrets of Human Intelligence Unlocked

articles.title: AI Voices Outperform Human Speech in Noisy Environments
articles.excerpt: A study reveals that AI voice clones are up to 20% easier to understand than human voices in noisy environments, suggesting AI "idealizes" speech for better clarity.

articles.title: Alzheimer’s Brains Share a Genetic Signature with Some Cancers
articles.excerpt: A study finds that Alzheimer’s is driven by cancer-like mutations in the brain's immune cells, which may enter from the blood and trigger lethal brain inflammation.

articles.title: Cellular Drawstrings Seal the Developing Brain
articles.excerpt: Researchers identified a "purse-string" mechanism powered by actin and molecular motors that physically pulls the neural tube closed during early development.

articles.title: SSRIs May Trigger Tinnitus
articles.excerpt: A new study identifies a specific serotonin circuit that triggers the auditory system, explaining why SSRIs can worsen tinnitus and offering a new target for treatment.
